context.projectOverview: Chat Interrupt / Approve / Steer (Phase 2). Sprint 3 = the WRITE path that closes the HITL loop. /approve <id> and /reject <id> [feedback] slash commands + NL approve/reject intent write .approved.json/.rejected.json markers by REUSING saveApproved/saveRejected + pendingExists guard + resolveApprover. The detached child's DiskCheckpointMechanism poll resumes automatically; reject feedback flows into the existing runCheckpointWithFeedback rework path. After resolve, the chat-owned RunState clears its pending fields and returns to 'running'.

completedSprints.summary: RunState grammar (input-required/paused + pending fields), CarefulSidecar, /careful, --approve-gates, RunSpawner careful spawn.

ApprovalReader (read-only over listPending), ApprovalCursor announce-once (key=checkpointId@requestedAt), handleTurn poll-prelude weaving notices into both reply paths, idempotent running->input-required RunState reflection, roster waiting=<gate>. Sprint 3 INVERTS that reflection on resolve and uses ApprovalReader to resolve an absent checkpoint id.

relevantFiles: src/state/approval-state.ts (saveApproved/saveRejected/pendingExists — REUSE, do NOT add write fns; capture exact ApprovedMarker/RejectedMarker shapes)
src/cli/commands/approve.ts (resolveApprover + pendingExists-before-write guard — export resolveApprover or replicate; approve.ts/reject.ts CLI tests must stay green)

src/orchestrator/checkpoints/mechanisms/disk.ts (poll/resume loop — reads <id>.approved.json/<id>.rejected.json -> {approved}/{approved:false,feedback}; do NOT modify; this is what proves chat markers integrate)
src/orchestrator/checkpoints/feedback-router.ts (the runCheckpointWithFeedback rework consumption path — verify, do NOT modify)
src/chat/chat-session.ts (handleStop pattern to mirror for handleApprove/handleReject; classify path routing; writeRunState to clear pending fields)
src/chat/slash-commands.ts (dispatch optional-handler threading + HELP_TEXT; /reject collects line remainder after checkpoint id as feedback)
src/chat/turn-classifier.ts (loose-JSON action union — extend with approve/reject {action,checkpointId?,feedback?}; jsonObjectMode)
src/chat/approval-reader.ts (resolve absent checkpoint id: single pending -> use it; multiple -> ASK)

keyInvariants: REUSE the approval store — do NOT reimplement the disk poll/resume (the detached child's DiskCheckpointMechanism already resumes when the marker appears). Chat only WRITES the marker + acks + clears RunState.

PENDINGEXISTS GUARD: mirror approve.ts — never write a dangling .approved.json/.rejected.json. /approve or /reject on a non-existent pending marker returns a clear 'no pending checkpoint' message and writes nothing.

NEVER GUESS A LOAD-BEARING TARGET: NL approve/reject with an absent checkpointId resolves via ApprovalReader — exactly ONE pending -> use it; MULTIPLE pending and none named -> return a clarifying question and write nothing.

RunState CLEAR ON RESOLVE: after writing the marker, clear pendingCheckpointId/pendingPrompt/pendingSince and set status back to 'running' on the correlated chat-owned RunState (inverse of Sprint 2's reflection). Idempotent; only act on input-required runs.

DO NOT MODIFY: approval-state.ts (except possibly EXPORTING resolveApprover), disk.ts, feedback-router.ts, or the existing approve.ts/reject.ts CLI behavior. Verify the feedback->rework path; don't change it.

CRITICAL TEST GOTCHA (curator): disk.ts DELETES stale markers at request()-start. The round-trip integration test (construct DiskCheckpointMechanism over the temp approvals dir, assert chat-written rejected marker resolves to {approved:false,feedback}) MUST write the marker via setTimeout AFTER mechanism.request() has begun polling — otherwise the start-of-request cleanup deletes it. Match the existing disk.test.ts timing pattern.
RejectedMarker.feedback exact field name must match what disk.ts returns in {approved:false, feedback} — verify the field name end-to-end.
successCriteriaSummary: sc-3-1 build; sc-3-2 typecheck; sc-3-3 tests pass incl new; sc-3-4 /approve existing pending -> writes .approved.json (approverId from resolveApprover) + ack; non-existent -> clear message, writes nothing (pendingExists guard); sc-3-5 /reject <id> <feedback> -> .rejected.json with feedback + DiskCheckpointMechanism resolves to {approved:false,feedback}; sc-3-6 NL approve/reject classified + routed; single-pending shortcut; ambiguous -> ASK, write nothing; sc-3-7 RunState clears pending + back to running; /help lists /approve and /reject.
principlesRef: .bober/principles.md (ESM/NodeNext, .js imports, no SDK types outside src/providers/, async fs, collocated *.test.ts, no fs mocks, loose-JSON classifier jsonObjectMode (DeepSeek-safe), conventional commit bober(sprint-3): ...)
notesForGenerator: Baseline after Sprint 2 is 2021 tests passing. There is a known PRE-EXISTING flake in src/mcp/tools/list-active-runs.test.ts (intermittent ENOTEMPTY cleanup race; passes on re-run/isolation) — do NOT try to fix it here (Sprint 6), but if your full-suite run shows ONLY that failure, re-run once to confirm green before handoff.
